Step 5: 	The Grand Offer, Part 1

Jon: 	Okay, let’s begin our tutorial presentation on step 5 of the 5-Step Video
Sales Letter Process. This is the grand offer.
Here are the 5 steps of the VSL process in order, just as a reminder.
You’ve got the snap suggestion, the vital connection, the big problem,
the bigger solution, and finally the grand offer. You should have that
completely memorized by now.
Alright, this is basically the part where you close the deal. You close
the deal and you reinforce their decision to buy. This is the grand
offer. This is the pitch, as some people call it. But this is so much
more than a sales pitch, as you’re about to discover. This goes way,
way beyond sales pitching.
I have to warn you, without this step, the other 4 steps are completely
in vain. You could have the greatest copy in the world, the most
awesome nightmare story in the world, your USP could be absolutely
stellar, everything could be awesome, your tips, everything. You fail at
the grand offer, and the video fails.
But that’s true of any sales page. It doesn’t matter if it’s a video sales
page or a text-based sales page, the offer is everything, and that is the
reason why most sales pages fail.
The benefit to doing a video sales letter pitch versus a pitch on a text-
based sales page will become very obvious in today’s tutorial.
So, check it out. It’s something you’ve got to get down.
This is basically what I say about this: this is where good video sales
letters become great. Like if you’ve written an okay video sales letter,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

2
you can make it great here. And the good thing about this is there’s
not a lot of writing skills involved here. It’s just sequencing. And well-
written video sales letters, like I said, can crash and burn.
So, we’ve divided the grand offer into 6 distinct parts. Part one is the
product. Part 2 is the price. Part 3 is the call to action, or the CTA.
Part 4 is what we call pain/pleasure. Part 5, actions, results. And part
6, the Q&A. Those are the 6 parts of the grand offer. We will be
covering all 6 parts in this video and in the following videos.
First things first. Open your mind map document for part one of the
grand offer. And in case you don’t have it in front of you, go to the 5-
Step VSL Process menu, click on the downloads tab, and grab it.
You’re going to want to have that in front of you as I go over the first
few sections of the grand offer.
So, in case you don’t have it, but you’re going to need it in front of you
because you’re going to want to make notes, here it is. This is the
flowchart for part one, the product.
This is the longest part of the video sales letter process, by the way,
the 5-Step VSL Process, part 5, the grand offer. This is the largest and
longest part of it. It looks daunting, but it’s not, because a lot of what
you see and hear, like this part here and this part here and this part
here, these are all literally like one slide, one slide. So we’ve kind of
broken it down.
Now, some of these are longer than that, but we’ve broken it down into
microscopic elements, so that you can see exactly what I’m doing
when I write a close, and exactly what the most successful video sales
letters that are out there are doing when they write their close. So, we
put all this together into a segment, I think, that’s going to be very
enlightening for you. And we’re going to start with the product.
Now, you’re going to begin with a simple reintroduction, and this is
assuming that you are narrating your video sales letter in the first
person. In other words, if I was narrating it, I would have started my
video sales letter by saying, “Hi, I’m Jon, and today I have an
interesting fill-in-the-blank,” weight loss story for you, make money
story for you.
So, this assumes that you’re doing that. And if you’re not doing that,
by the way, obviously this doesn’t work. You’re just going to want to
introduce the product itself.
But if you are voicing this and saying, “Hi, I’m Lisa,” or “Hi, I’m
George,” or whatever this is where you’re going to want to feel free to
use your last name, if that’s okay with your privacy policies, in this
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 19 --

3
introduction, because you are now shifting gears. You’re shifting gears
from a casual mode of talking to a close mode. We’re going from
casual, despite the whole nightmare story and going through the pain
and the pleasure. It’s still in a casual tone, because you’re still talking
to them as a one-name person, without a system, without the name of
the system. We’re going into close mode.
So, at this point, you want to introduce yourself. You want to
reconnect yourself, and you want to do it to the product or the service,
if this is the case. In other words, if you have the option to do this.
If not, you want to simply go with the introduction that you’ve already
done in the final step of slide 4.
So, in other words, if you’re speaking for a company and you’re not
introducing yourself as a person or as a guru to go to, then you’re just
going to want to use what you’ve already written in the final slide of
step 4.
But let’s say that you are speaking in first person. This is what you
would say: “Hi, I’m Jon Benson, creator of the Every Other Day Diet.”
Wow, that was pretty simple, right? That was the entire introduction,
so we’ll go back here and take another look at that. All I said is my first
and last name.
If I wanted to, I could list credentials here. “Hi, I’m Jon Benson, a
masters degree in blank, and I’ve done this in blank, and a nutritionist
in blank. I’m the creator of the Every Other Day Diet.”
Well, next, you want to include your USP. That’s the very next thing
you want to do. If you’re following along with the flowchart, you will
see exactly where that’s going through the mind map. You’re going to
include your USP and you’re going to connect this to your product
specifically. This is very important.
Here’s what I mean by that, here’s an example slide. By the way, I
have example slides for every single segment, every part. In short,
you’re getting an entire close with a video sales letter over these next
few videos.
So, an example slide of this would be, “The Every Other Day Diet. It’s
the first lifestyle-friendly diet plan ever created that allows you to eat
your favorite foods and still lose all the weight you want.”
If you will remember back from the USP factory videos, this is my USP.
All I’ve done here is added the words, “The Every Other Day Diet.” All
you would do is to add the words, “The name of my product or service
is USP.” Now you see, yet again, why we started with the USP factory,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 19 --

4
and how important the USP is. It’s used continually through a video
sales letter.
But now you’re wanting to say, “Here is the overall big-picture element.
There’s one thing that I want you to believe. This is the one thing I
want you to believe.”
And if you go into a video sales letter close asking your viewer to
believe more than one thing, you will bomb. All I’m asking them to
believe, again, we’ve walked through this with the USP factory. If you
don’t remember it, go look at the videos. Hopefully, you’ve done the
homework. All I’m doing is saying, “This is the one thing I’m asking
you to believe, that you can eat your favorite foods and still lose all the
weight you want.”
Now, obviously, this is applied to my particular product. You would
apply it to yours. You would use the name of your product and your
USP here. That’s all there is to that part.
Next, you want to begin the process of answering objections. These
are like the things that people may be thinking in the back of their
head, saying, “Yeah, but maybe it won’t work for me.” You want to do
this before they’re even raised. Because, at this point, your prospect is
not quite there yet. It’s kind of starting to enter their mind, because
they’re starting to get the idea, “Okay, there’s a product here, but...”
And this is what we’re going to do. We’re going to cancel out all those
buts before they even happen, and we’re going to do that by using
what I call the “even if” technique. The “even if” technique. It’s so
simple. I’m going to give you an example of this.
“This radically simple diet.” Now, notice how I used power words.
Radically simple. I could have said, “This diet will work for you.” But
again, I want you to work through wordsmithing. Go back through that
wordsmithing video. “This radically simple diet will work for you, even
if…” and this is where I go into the even if, “even if you have,” and I list
a whole lot of things that people would say are objections.
And I could have kept going here, but this is just an example. “A
thyroid condition, if you have bad genetics, if you’ve a body that gains
weight easily, if you have children.” And then I go, “Yeah, mommies
can get flat tummies, too.” I go through every single objection they
may have, that I can think of.
And then, I get to the big one: “or if you think you’ve tried everything.”
This slide should be in every single video sales letter, in my opinion. “If
you think you’ve tried everything. You might think you’ve tried
everything, but you haven’t.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 19 --

5
So I want to give you a very important tip on this slide. Most people,
and that includes myself, have an idea of what our clients’ objections
are to our products. But guess what? Most of the time, you’re wrong.
Most of the time, you’re wrong.
So, here’s what you do to find out what those objections should be. It’s
very, very simple. If you have a list of clients, you want to survey your
list and say – and this is going to be interesting – “What was the one
thing or 2 things or 3 things that were holding you back before you
made the decision to purchase blank?” whatever your product or
service is. “This is going to help me improve my service in the future.
Thank you very much.” Maybe offer them a gift.
Then, you want to go to 10 or 20 of your most trusted friends and
colleagues in business and say, “I would like you, if you wouldn’t mind,
to write down 10 reasons or 5 reasons or 3 reasons you would not buy
my product.” Get other people’s opinions.
Now, you’re going to have a lot of good opinions. Like, for example, no
one in my immediate circle would have said, “A thyroid condition,”
because they just don’t know that. But they might have said, “Price,”
for example. Now, no one did, in my case. “Oh, $47 is too expensive.”
Well, it’s not.
So, price didn’t come up. But price may come up for you, and you may
not be aware of that.
Or what may come up for you is like, “I just don’t know if your service
will ever follow through.” Maybe there’s a doubt of service, those kind
of things.
When you put that in here, when you know that information, you can
put it right here in the objections. So, it’s so easy to do, once you
understand what it is. But just always end with, “If you think you’ve
tried everything.”
Okay, next you want to explain what your product or service is not,
what it isn’t. And here, you’re going to accomplish 2 things. First of all,
you’re going to set up the following sequence of slides, where you kick
out the tire-kickers, as I say. You don’t want these clients. You just
don’t want them. You want to narrow it down to the clients you want.
I fire clients all the time. I will fire clients. If I don’t want to work with
somebody, if I think that they’re too much of a pain in the butt to work
with or if I think that they’re just not going to get the results because
they’re not going to apply themselves, I don’t want them as a client.
Just don’t want them. It’s not a win-win.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 19 --

6
So this is where you’re going to kick out the tire-kickers. And it’s a very
good thing to do, because of this reason. You’re psychologically doing
something else. You’re clearly differentiating your product or service
from your competition, because you’re going to talk about all the lower-
rated, cheesy, non-effective options. You’re also doing one other thing
I’ll cover in a minute, that’s really powerful. But this is a fun way to do
this, and I’ll show you some example slides.
I’ll say, “Now.” Notice how I begin with an anchor drop or a power
word, “now.” “Let me tell you what the Every Other Day Diet is not, just
so you know exactly what you’re about to experience.”
Notice what I’ve done here, wordsmithing. I’m going to stop in these
slides and just show you some wordsmithing tricks.
“Just so you know exactly what” – I highlighted the word “exactly” –
you’re about to experience…” I am future-pacing them to what they’re
about to experience. I am assuming the sale already. “You’re about to
experience this. So, let me tell you what it isn’t, just to make sure
we’re on the same page. This is not yet another fad diet or Hollywood
starvation plan. This is not some swallow this magic pill solution that
promises something silly, like lose 50 pounds in 50 minutes, or
something ridiculous like that.”
And this is where I come back and I tie it all together. I’ll say, “You’ve
been lied to enough.”
Now go back to what? Where are we going with the lies? We’re going
to the tips. Remember your 3 tips? Go to your 3 tips. You will find the
lies that people believe, and you want to reinforce the fact that, “Hey, I
just covered all this. You’ve been lied to enough. It’s time for a
solution that works without the gimmicks. And this is definitely not a
starve-and-feast approach that can harm your health.”
Now, that was very important for my particular product, because Every
Other Day Diet sounds like something like where you starve one day
and pig out the next, and that’s not what this is. So, I want to make
sure they understand what it isn’t before I tell them what it is.
So now, you want to tell your prospect about what I call the hero study.
This is a very powerful way to set your product up on a pedestal that is
so high, people are going to be clamoring for it. Services work, as
well.
I’m going to say this right off the bat. You may not have a hero study.
In other words, it may be a brand new product. This may have been
written off the top of your head, with no expertise, no interviews, no
backing, whatever. And it may be a service that doesn’t really involve
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 19 --

7
a hero study. If you own a dry cleaners for example, you may not have
a hero study. If you do, this is a fantastic way to position your product.
If you don’t, it’s not a make-or-break, so don’t worry about that.
So, check out what this is. This is merely saying that you’ve analyzed
experts, studied the best of the best, discovered trends and patterns,
and so forth.
And that part would work for any service. I’ll take dry cleaners, for
example. You might say, “Look, I’ve looked at 55 of the top dry
cleaners in the nation, and this is what they do best.” That’s just a
hero study. That’s all it is. You’re looking at the heroes in your
industry.
Now, this concludes with this very important mantra: “Their success
can be your success.” Okay? “Taking all the successful people that
I’ve interviewed and all these heroes, I figured out what made them
successful and I can make you successful using the same thing.”
Here’s some example slides. I begin with “rather,” because, again, if
you look through this in the actual PowerPoint file, you’ll see that this
all flows very nicely. “Rather, the Every Other Day Diet is the result of
over 10 years of study.” So it’s not a fad diet. I just went through what
it isn’t, remember? “Analyzing the most successful, long-term weight
loss case studies ever, as well as looking at the real reasons behind
diet failures, and creating a clever solution to the age-old problem of
lifestyle-cramping, boring diet programs. And now starting today” –
notice I’m future pacing again – “starting today, their success can be
your success too.”
So, I’ve already made the case that these are the most successful
dieters in the world, that I’ve analyzed this for 10 years. I’ve figured
out the patterns to their success, and it can be their success, too.
They can replicate their success. It’s role-modeling.
So now, you want to explain the result of the hero study, because all I
did is say, in this case, I looked at this, I analyzed this. But what was
the result? What did I come up with?
I gave a very vague description of the hero study. I just set the tone for
telling them more.
And then, you want to connect these results to your product or service
through the use of power words and power phrases. Here you want to
emphasize benefits over features. Let’s not talk about the features of
your product yet. You’re not there yet. You’re only in the benefit
phase. Notice I haven’t said one thing about the features. Not one
thing.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 19 --

8
Big mistake people make when they enter a pitch zone, when they
enter the grand offer, as I call it, is they start talking about how great
their product is. No one cares. No one cares. They want to know how
great the benefits are that your product offers, or service.
So, here are some example slides.
“The success patterns I studied and successfully duplicated” – that’s
kind of a cool way of saying it, so you can just take that language, if
you want – “give you the freedom.”
Now, I’ve got 2 powerful words here: you and freedom. Again, what
are the top 3 things that people want? You have to look at this. They
want release from pain, they want freedom, and they want love. Those
are the big ones. So, I continually emphasize freedom.
“This is going to give you the freedom to easily build a tasty diet plan
around your favorite healthy foods, along with your favorite cheat
foods, all while your energy soars, your weight loss begins to steadily
decrease, and you finally reclaim the life and body you absolutely
deserve.”
Notice how those slides were completely beneficial-driven. We’ll go
back here and look at this. “You can easily build a tasty diet plan. You
can include your favorite cheat foods. You can do this while you’re
energy soars, your weight begins to decrease. You can reclaim your
life.” These are benefits. It has nothing to do with one aspect of my
product, as far as a physical aspect, as far as saying, “Okay the caloric
staggering principle, and I’m going to give you these 6 modules and
these 6 videos.” Nothing. I’m just telling them what to expect.
Now, we’re going to twist the knife a little bit here. We’re going to
close the door to the tire-kickers, at this point. We’re going to shut
them down. Remember, this whole segment started off with let’s kick
out the tire-kickers? Well, now we’re going to really do it. This is, I told
you, that third thing.
There was a third thing that I said is psychologically even more
powerful reason to do this. Here we get to that third thing. This is
going to build even greater rapport with your prospects who have
connected to your message, which are the only ones you want to sell
to anyway, because of this fact: everyone wants to be a member of an
exclusive club. Not a club that allows anyone in.
So, if a club goes, “Yeah, we’ll let anyone in. It doesn’t matter.” Like if
for example some college gave away an academic awards club or
whatever, and they said, “Yeah, if your GPA is over 1.0, you can get
in.” Well, most people would go, “I don’t want to be in that club.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 19 --

9
So if you’re going to let anyone in the club, you want to make sure,
“No, this is for people with 3.5 GPA’s and higher.” Oh, okay, well that’s
a little bit more exclusive. And this is how you want to position your
product or service.
You want to make sure that you’re getting the client that you want,
you’re not getting some yahoo that thinks that you’re going to push a
button and make $1-million and those kind of things and, at the same
time, you’re going to make the people who are already connected to it,
that are going, “Yeah, this sounds really good. Oh, wow, this is
exclusive, too,” it’s going to make them feel very proud. It’s going to
make them feel very “cherished” is a good word to say. I am taking
care of the people who are the most important that are listening to me.
And this is very powerful.
Some example slides. I’ll say, “Before we go any further, it’s only fair
that I be absolutely 100% honest with you.” Notice the wording here,
the wordsmithing. “Absolutely”: power word. “Honest.” Again, people
love honesty. “100% honest with you. 100% honest with you.”
“If.” Notice how I bolded the word if. We’ll get into that. It’s called
punching. “If you are looking for that magic pill weight loss answer, or
if you want just another 10-day belly blast diet fad, or some other plan
that you already know will never, ever work for you…”
Now, notice what I’ve done here. I’ve basically covered every single
plan you can think of except mine. It’s kind of cool because I’ve, first of
all, knocked down all of the magical solutions, the weight loss pills that
don’t work, and the 10-day liquid diets or belly blast – I just made that
up – diet fads, or some other plan that you already know will never,
ever work for you.
By the way. in my Word document where I read this off. I will have this
slide annotated for a paraphrase. Remember, you want to paraphrase
every 5 to 10, maybe 15 slides. I will paraphrase this slide. Let me
show you what I mean by that.
I will read around it. Even though there’s more words on the screen
than a paraphrase slide usually is, it’s because we’re in the close. In
the close, you usually want most the words on the screen.
So, I’ll say something like this: “Or some other plan that you already
know will never, ever work for you. You know those plans that you’ve
tried before. You know those diets, the fad diets. They never will work
for you. They never have worked for you, have they? They’ve gotten
you exactly where you are right now. And you know what? If that’s
what you want, leave this page right now.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 19 --

10
I literally tell someone to leave the page. I mean because at this point
that is a complete pattern interrupt, that’s a complete shocking
statement. So I’m saying, “If this is what you want, leave.”
And I’ve actually carried this further. I’ve said, “You know, if that’s
what you want, leave this page right now and go to this page:
FantasyLife.com, because what you’re wanting to do is live in a fantasy
world. And this is not about fantasy, this is about reality.”
You want to know something? The people you lose, you would never
sell, ever, in a million years. And if you did, they would refund your
product. So, who cares?
The people that are listening, that are going, “Yeah, this is pretty cool.
This is pretty cool.” If they were on the fence, they are over the fence
now. They are ready to buy the product. This is what makes this
technique so powerful.
You’ve won over the honesty factor, and people love to trust. They
really want to trust you. They do. At this point, you’ve just increased
the trust.
So, here’s where I tell them what the diet is. “This diet is for men and
women.” Notice the inclusion statement here. So, if you have a
product, you say, “This product is for anyone blank to blank.” If it’s an
age-specific, you want to list it here. If it’s gender-specific, you want to
say, “This is for men only,” or “women only.”
In my case, it’s not specific at all. I just say, “For men and women who
are ready to put down the miracle answers, yet still want the most tasty
way to achieve long-term weight loss.
“In short, there are no easy answers. You’ve been lied to in the past.
People tell you this is easy.” See, I’m paraphrasing. “But you know
what? You know that if weight loss was easy, you would have already
done it. But here’s the deal: I’ve made it as easy as it can possibly
ever get. Isn’t that awesome?”
That is what people want to hear. So, if you have a product or service
that you’ve made more efficient, that still requires work – and, honestly,
3X VSL is a perfect example of this, 3X VSL requires work. I talk
about this when I speak live, when I speak on webinars. I say, “Look, if
you’re not willing to do the work, leave now because this isn’t going to
work for you. You actually have to get out and do some typing and
actually go through the videos.” Duh, right? I mean it doesn’t pop out
of nowhere. So you have to do the work, but I’ve made it as easy as it
can possibly get. I’ve made this as easy as anyone’s ever made it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 19 --

11
That is going to win over just about everyone.
And I conclude by this. “So, if you’re wanting the real answer to weight
loss.” Please use that slide. “So if you’re wanting the real answer to
Forex. If you’re wanting the real answer to making money online. If
you’re wanting the real answer to how to throw a party,” – you can use
this for any service or product – “then here’s what you can expect.”
Notice how I say, “Here’s what you can expect.” It’s future-pacing. I’m
taking them right down the line, as if they’ve already decided to buy the
product. “Here’s what you can expect.”
So now, you want to qualify the remaining prospects by taking them
down the road that is the “if you want to” path. That’s what I say: “If
you want to.” At this point, you’re going to do another sort of pattern
interrupt by having rapid succession slides.
If you want to paraphrase these slides, by the way, it’s okay to do it. I
did not do it in this example. I actually have every word on the slide,
but the slides are short.
And the rapid succession of the slides creates a slight hypnotic affect,
and you’re just going benefit, feature, benefit, feature, benefit, feature.
So, basically, we’ve now narrowed it down to people who are wanting
a real solution, the real answer to this. And I said, “If you want the real
answer to this.” And I tie this back in too. “And if you want to drop
10% of your unwanted body weight in less than 30 days, if you want to
enjoy your favorite foods while getting leaner and healthier, if you want
to put an end to low energy, low drive, afternoon fatigue, and get the
body you desire without sacrificing your entire lifestyle to do it, then the
Every Other Day Diet isn’t just a system for you, it is the only system
for you.”
So, we go from if, then, only. This is how this little section concludes.
We’re qualifying the remaining viewers. “This is the only system for
you.”
In case that sounds familiar, as it should, believe it or not, it was
covered in the USP factory. It was covered in a lot of the earlier videos
where I said, “You do not want to have an answer for somebody, you
want to have the only answer for somebody.” This is how you want to
position it.
So, I said, “If you want this, this, this, and this,” and notice that my last
this happened to be part of my USP. “This is what makes my product
really unique, without sacrificing your entire lifestyle to do it, then this
isn’t just a system for you, it is the only system for you.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 19 --

12
You could say an answer for you it’s the only answer for you. I like the
word system, formula, blueprint, etc, but you can use the power word
of your choice.
That’s a very powerful introduction into this next segment. And this is
the testimonials. Notice we haven’t had any graphics to this point.
This is where we’re going to start bringing in graphics and we’re going
to start bringing in graphics also later on, product graphics, etc.
But you don’t have to go graphic crazy in a video sales letter. I use
very minimal graphics.
Now, in this first part, we’re going to use testimonials. So it’s always
good to use a visual shot of a testimonial. If you have a short video,
like 20 seconds long, you play that within your video sales letter. You
just want to make sure that you limit these to 3 or 4, because we’re
going to use testimonials sprinkled throughout your video sales letter,
and some in some really strategic places that most people don’t use
them, that have been shown to radically increase conversions.
So, don’t throw all your testimonials in here at once.
Here’s some example slides. I’m just going to go through this real
quick. I would paraphrase these slides. You don’t have to. You don’t
have to at all, but I would.
So I’ve got the quote from Mario down here. I’d say something to this
effect. “Just check out the results that average people are getting.
These are average, everyday, normal people like Mario. He lost 100
pounds since he started the EODD system. 100 pounds! He wanted
that skinny body. He’s been obese all of his life and he wanted that
real skinny body, that runner’s body. That was his choice, and look
how much weight he dropped, eating his favorite foods.
“And then a lot of times, when women have babies, they really
complain about losing their tummies. They complain about getting the
bigger thighs and all that stuff. Well, look what Angie did. Angie did
this in 6 weeks, using Every Other Day Diet. She’s got feminine abs
now, not the big, bulky, bodybuilder woman. But look at those. It’s a
nice 6-pack, with 2 kids. She’s had 2 kids, has a body like that.”
“And here’s a guy who’s almost 60 years old. He’s over 60. And in
just 3 weeks, look at the body fat coming off his stomach. This is a 3
week old picture. The pictures speak for themselves.”
So, that’s how I would read through my testimonials. I would get very
excited, I would make them very personal. I wouldn’t read them word-
for-word, unless they’re just totally awesome. And even then, I would
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 19 --

13
start skipping. I would start reading just the big parts of it and then I
would accentuate those parts of it. This is called reading through the
copy, by the way. It’s pretty easy to do.
Now, you want to cover, at this point, your product or service specifics.
We have not done that, to this point. Now, after the testimonials, after
you’ve weeded out everyone that you don’t want to buy your product,
after you’ve really got your core audience going, “Oh, my gosh, this is
so freaking awesome. Tell me more about it,” now they know the
benefits.
You see the mistake that most marketers make? They go into all
about it before they go into all about what it delivers. Well, hopefully
through this course, you’ve learned that that is exactly backwards. I’ve
covered all that it delivers. We’re going to keep doing that, by the way.
But now, let’s get into some specifics. Now, this is where you’re going
to want anything specific to your service or product, and you’re going
to use some product images, if you have them. More will come in
later. And this also an optional place to drop the buy button.
Now, there’s already a tutorial called “Magic Buy Button,” and it’s going
to be in the Completing Your VSL section. So you’re going to want to
look over that tutorial.
So, don’t worry about that, right now. If you don’t know what a magic
buy button is, I’m actually going to show you in here what this is. But
most people do. This is the very first time this could show up.
I have tested both, and I encourage you to test both as well. And when
I say both, what I mean is having the buy button show up here, versus
having the buy button show up when you deliver the price, which is
further down the video sales letter chain. You need to test both.
In fact, I test having a buy button below the video during the entire
time, although 9 times out of 10 that fails. But you never know. It’s a
good test.
So, this is the first place that you might want to have this drop down
right after those testimonials and you’re getting into the specifics of the
product. A lot of people are ready to buy, at this point.
Finally, during this section, you’re going to want to include any of those
coined phrases that we talked about. Remember coined phrases?
Remember going back to your USP – amazing how everything goes
back to that – your primary benefit and the driving force behind your
primary benefit. Go back and watch those videos again. If you haven’t
done the homework, please do it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 19 --

14
I asked you to coin a phrase, if possible. Coin a phrase. You can coin
more than one phrase; something unique, that makes your system
sound really kind of cool. Basically, we’re trying to make it sound cool.
And this is going to follow along with the, “It’s the only way to achieve
blank. The only way to get this.” Again, it’s not the way, it’s the only
way is to achieve this with your product or service.
That’s why coined phrases are so cool, is because you coin a phrase,
“This is the only system in the world that has this.” See why it’s so
powerful? You can say, “It’s the only system in the world that has this.”
Now, here’s some example slides to help you wrap your head around
it. “Take a look at what you will receive inside your copy of The Every
Other Day Diet.” Again, I accented the words “take a look.” And you
could use something else, like you could say, “What you’re about to
receive,” “what you’re going to get today inside your copy of The Every
Other Day Diet.”
But look how specific I get. “On page 17,” I tell them on what page it’s
going to be, “you’ll discover the foundations of my caloric staggering
principle.” Trademark coined phrase. Primary benefit, driving force
right here. “You’ll discover the foundations of my caloric staggering
principle. This includes the exact timing and strategy for how you use
your favorite foods to encourage more rapid fat loss.”
Right out of the gate, I hit them with the number one benefit. Wow,
boom. And this is a benefit couched inside of a product-specific thing.
So, I tell them on this page, here’s what you’re going to read. It’s
product-specific. But what’s it going to do for you? It’s going to
encourage more rapid fat loss.
“On page 32 through 38, you’ll discover the 3 EODD diet plans, and
which plan is best for your specific body type. You’ll see plans for
beginners, intermediate dieters, and even the advanced athlete
nutrition experts. Everyone is covered here, folks.” So I’ll paraphrase
that slide a little bit.
“You’ll find our complete Every Other Day Diet super fast recipe guide
on page 72. And on page 91,” this is where the only way really comes
in powerful, folks, “on page 91, you’ll be introduced to the only way to
keep your weight off permanently. And this is the method that has
worked for virtually everyone who has applied it. You simply cannot
get this anywhere else.”
So, at this point, I have sold the product, basically. Anyone who’s
interested in weight loss is going to be interested in this.
But what you want to do next is you want to emphasize any aspects of
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 19 --

15
your product or services that deal with the following things: speed,
efficiency, service, ease of use, and you also want to include any
images that work as pattern interrupts. I’m going to give you some
specific slide examples.
“As you probably noticed by now.” By the way, that’s a great way to
start off this segment. “As you’ve noticed by now, or probably noticed
by now, the Every Other Day Diet is, by far, the easiest diet in the
world to follow.”
Even if they haven’t noticed that by now, I’m going to say it for them
anyway. “It’s the easiest diet in the world to follow,” period, “as it
allows you to consume your favorite foods frequently, preventing
boredom from setting in, and allowing you to get greater lifestyle
freedom. And the results come fast, too. In fact, many EODD’ers
report that they drop 10% of their unwanted body weight in less than
30 short days.”
What have I done here? I’m emphasizing speed and ease. I did that
in 2 slides. Speed and ease.
“And that is amazing, considering they are seeing these results and
experiencing this transformation.”
Now, before I go to the next slide, seeing, experiencing, what am I
focusing on? I’m focusing on feelings and I’m focusing on senses.
Seeing, experiencing. This is what people want. They’re seeing and
experiencing this.
“Transforming while eating delicious pizza, pecan pie, burgers and
fries. Your favorite food goes here, folks. Whatever you enjoy the
most.”
Now, at that point, obviously, I’m paraphrasing that because there’s no
copy on the slide. But I’m using images as a pattern interrupt. This is
not interrupting a pattern giving them something they don’t expect. In
a sense it is, because they’ve seen so much type on the screen. Then
they see images. This is merely getting their minds and mouth
connected, in this case.
So, this is where you would get their mind and their eyes connected. If
you’re selling a bed and breakfast, you would show pictures of that. If
you’re selling a service-oriented thing, you would show pictures of that.
If you’re selling something that increases security, you would show
images of people who look secure.
So, again, these don’t have to be specific images to your product.
They can just be images that convey a feeling.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 19 --

16
Now, we’re going to move into the first price tease. Here is where I’m
going to hint at a price. I’m going to obviously let the user know that
there is a cost to this program, but we’re not even going to tell them
what it is yet. We’re just going to hint at it.
An optional technique is to hint at what the price is not, by giving a
relative comparison. But that’s an advanced technique, and I don’t
even think that you need to do that. But I wanted to toss that in there,
because a lot of video sales letters have that.
Now, here’s what I say. “Now,” I begin with an anchor drop, the word
now, power word, “you may believe.” So what have I accented here?
Believe. Because your subconscious mind is going to see one word,
“believe.” It’s a perfect thing to put in right after I show pizza and
pecan pie, right? Believe.
So, “Now, you may believe that a system that can do all this for you
pretty much will cost a pretty penny. And in all honesty, it really
should. After all, this system has helped thousands of folks just like
you drop tens of thousands of pounds, quickly. I’m going to get to the
price in just a few moments.”
Now, what have I done here? I’ve opened a loop. I’ve hinted at a
price. I’ve opened a loop that I’m going to close, obviously later, with
the price.
But before I do that, I want to say, “First,” because this is how you get
out of a loop, “first, I want to share a few other unique benefits this
one-of-a-kind system is going to deliver to you.”
Notice what I’ve done here. I have said it’s going to cost something,
and “I’m going to get to that price in just a minute. But first.” And when
you say, “But first,” or in my case when I say, “However, first,” or just
first, because I don’t like to use the word “but,” I’ll say, “First,” and I will
pause briefly. That causes a psychological breach. It’s a good breach.
You want someone to be slightly interrupted. It’s like snapping your
fingers. So I’ll say, “First, I want to share a few other unique benefits
this one-of-a-kind system will deliver to you.”
Now, I want to emphasize just a couple other wordsmithing things.
This is kind of a cross-tutorial between creating the grand offer and
wordsmithing the hell out of it.
“The one-of-a-kind system,” I’m using one-of-a-kind right out of the
power phrases, “unique,” power word, “will deliver to you.”
Why did I choose “will deliver to you?” Because oftentimes, the reason
why takeout services work so well, the reason why done-for-you
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 19 --

17
products work so well is because people want something delivered to
them.
Look don’t make me work too much for this. I want something
delivered to me. Well, I’ve already narrowed down the people, I’ve
already kicked them out, the people that think it’s a push-button thing.
They’re gone. So, at this point, I want to emphasize, “I’m going to
deliver this to you.” Okay, it doesn’t mean I’m going to deliver you a
lean, fit body. That’s impossible. “I’m going to deliver the system to
you. I’m going to give it to you.” So, this is a neat way of saying that.
So, finally, we’re going to cover 3 more benefits using guess what?
Avoid, enjoy, and how to. Where have you heard that before? The 3
tips. Now, again, you see why we do this first, the 3 tips, because they
play out through the entire video sales letter.
Now, the good news is you’re only going to use this over one slide
each, so you don’t have to worry about going through the tip model
again. Don’t worry about that. This is super-short and sweet.
So, here’s some example slides.
“You’ll discover the 11 diet lies you absolutely must avoid at all costs,
in order to see the results you desire. And that’s covered on pages 22
through 35.”
So, what have I done here? Look at what I’ve done. “You’ll discover,”
power word. “The 11.” I’ve got specific with numerology, not
numerology as the philosophy, but numerology, numbers. I’ve got a
number here, “11 diet lies that you absolutely must avoid.” So, what is
this? This is an avoid tip. But I don’t tell them what the tip is. You
don’t have to here, you’re just going to tell them that it’s in the book or
in the product or in the service, whatever your avoid tip is.
And if possible, say where it is. If it’s a service, obviously you can’t say
where it is unless you say, “Our customer service rep will cover that
with you during your first day,” or whatever. But I’m going to say, “You
have to avoid this in order to blank.” In other words, “Avoid these diet
lies.” Why? Because you want to see the results you desire. I mean it
could be just that simple.
Notice the word desire. “The results you want” is good, “results you
desire,” better. “That’s covered on pages 22 through 35.” Specifics.
“You’ll also discover some really clever ways.” I love the word “clever,”
because that kind of insinuates that they’re clever for taking you up on
it. “Really clever ways to make meal prep and scheduling an absolute
blast.” Where am I at now? Enjoy tip. I decided to make this one 2
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 19 --

18
slides.
“You know, I’ve proven that dieting can be and should be enjoyable.” I
actually used the word enjoyable, right in the presentation. “And I want
to share those secrets with you, too.”
Now, I say, “You too.” Why? Now this is not make-or-break stuff. This
is just clever wordsmithing. I say, “You too.” That implies what? That
other people already know this. Again, you do not want to have a
feeling of exclusion, you want to deliver a feeling of inclusion. So,
when you say, “You too,” you are automatically psychologically
creating a state of inclusion. Cool stuff.
“Plus.” Notice that huge anchor drop. This is one of the few times I’ll
actually change typefaces during anchor drops. And anchor drops can
occur before or after, like at the top or bottom of a slide. “I’ll be
revealing exactly how to.” What tip is this? How to. “Eat your favorite
foods, and the specific down to the hour times when you should eat
them in order to accelerate fat burning.”
Now, you may think to yourself, “Haven’t I just heard that before?” You
bet you have. I just reworded something I’ve already said.
Redundancy is okay in a video sales letter. It’s perfectly okay,
because the redundancy in this point, in this illustration, is my USP.
You cannot overemphasize your USP, unless you get absurd and say
it every third slide or something.
I will be redundant with my USP and specifically with my USP. I’ll be
redundant about a few other things, but definitely the USP.
And notice the word that I’m using: revealing. “Revealing” is an
awesome word. I use it all the time. It’s in the power words list.
Okay, I’ve covered the 3 benefits that I want to cover. And I’ve
narrowed it down to the 3 tip structures: avoid, enjoy, and how to. I
could have used just 3 slides. I chose to use I think 5.
So, in other words, this isn’t long. This is just what about your product
or service can they enjoy. What about your product or service they
need to avoid, in other words to achieve the results they want. And
you’ve got to give them a how-to. Even if that how-to has already been
covered in your video sales letter, it does not matter. If it is, notice the
words “specific, down to the hour times.” That’s huge. That’s huge.
Because remember in my video sales letter, up to this point, in my
example video sales letter that I’ve given you. If you don’t remember
this, you want to look at the previous video, and you will find exactly
what I’m talking about.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 19 --

19
In my sample video sales letter, I say, “Now, you’ve got to know when
to eat these foods. You can’t just do it at any time. You’ve got to know
specifically when. I’m going to cover how to do that later. And this is
how. You have to buy the product.”
So now, I’ve closed that loop. I’ve said, “Okay, this is specifically down
to the hour times when you need to do this. And it’s going to
accelerate your fat burning.”
